channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Oscar Piastri axed by McLaren ahead of crucial Hungarian Grand Prix testing
item.description: The article discusses the decision by McLaren to ax Oscar Piastri ahead of the crucial Hungarian Grand Prix testing. Piastri, a young Australian driver, was part of McLaren's test program, but the tea...

item.title: Floods in Gilgit-Baltistan Disrupt Travel, Damage Infrastructure
item.description: Torrential rains in Gilgit-Baltistan have caused widespread travel disruptions, with flash floods and landslides washing away roads and bridges, leaving thousands stranded. The Pakistan Meteorological...

item.title: Treefrog mating strategy boosts nest survival through male collaboration
item.description: African grey foam-nest treefrogs (Chiromantis xerampelina) display unique reproductive behaviors where females mate with multiple males to improve nest quality. Research shows that polyandry, once tho...

item.title: Wildfire-Generated Pyrocumulus Clouds Observed Over British Columbia
item.description: A wildfire near Anderson Creek in British Columbia has produced massive pyrocumulus clouds, visible as cauliflower-like formations hundreds of kilometers away. The Anderson Creek fire was discovered b...

item.title: Cardi B Reacts to Rumors About Her Relationship With Nigerian Footballer Maduka Okoye
item.description: Cardi B has addressed rumors about her relationship with Nigerian goalkeeper Maduka Okoye after photos of them together in Italy sparked a media frenzy. The situation escalated when Okoye's baby mama,...

item.title: Governor Bago Urges Fulani Community To Work With Security Agencies Against Criminals In Niger State
item.description: Niger State Governor, Umaru Mohammed Bago, has called on members of the Fulani community to cooperate fully with security agencies as part of efforts to tackle insecurity across the state. Speaking du...

item.title: Andrea Johnson faces questioning over inconsistent testimony before the Madlanga Commission
item.description: Head of the Independent Directorate Against Corruption (IDAC), Advocate Andrea Johnson, came under sustained questioning before the Madlanga Commission of Inquiry after inconsistencies emerged between...

item.title: Class Suspensions Due to Tropical Depression Kiyapo and Southwest Monsoon
item.description: Schools in several regions of the Philippines have suspended classes for Friday, July 24, 2026, due to heavy rains caused by Tropical Depression Kiyapo and the southwest monsoon. The affected areas in...

item.title: Lack of School Facilities in Housing Projects Exacerbates Classroom Shortage in Philippines
item.description: The Second Congressional Commission on Education (EDCOM 2) reported that 58 National Housing Authority (NHA) housing projects lack planned education facilities, worsening classroom shortages in the Ph...

item.title: Trump Faces Criticism for Unilateral Changes to Saudi Nuclear Deal Without Consultation
item.description: The article details how White House press secretary Karine Leavitt confirmed President Donald Trump's unilateral modifications to the U.S.-Saudi Arabia nuclear deal through his Truth Social platform, ...

item.title: Modern Minimalist Renovation Balances Texture and Warmth in Kitchener Home
item.description: A Kitchener family transformed their 10-year-old home from a 1990s-style property into a modern minimalist space that blends elegance with comfort. The renovation, led by design firm Reigo & Bauer, fo...

item.title: Why Neptune Has the Fastest Winds in the Solar System
item.description: Neptune, the eighth planet in our solar system, exhibits the fastest winds of any planet, reaching speeds over 2,000 kilometers per hour. Despite its distance from the Sun—4.5 billion kilometers away,...

item.title: Centre invites NEET paper leak protesters for talks with JP Nadda as government reiterates willingness for dialogue
item.description: Union Minister of State Jitendra Singh has invited the protesters demonstrating against the NEET paper leak issue for discussions with the Central government at the residence or office of Union Health...

item.title: Canada's leaders unite against U.S. trade threats amid tariff tensions
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney and Canadian premiers have emphasized the importance of unity in response to U.S. President Donald Trump's threat to impose 50% tariffs on Canadian goods starting August 19....

item.title: Kenyan Journalist Compares Politicians to Historical 'Bearded Sisters' Group
item.description: Veteran journalist Jeff Koinange compared Senator Kaba Methu and newly elected Ol Kalou MP to the legendary 'Bearded Sisters' during a Sunday Live interview. The label refers to a group of seven outsp...

item.title: Rapid Nuclear Reactor Construction and Safety Concerns Under Trump's Deregulation Push
item.description: President Trump's initiative to accelerate nuclear reactor construction, particularly small modular reactors, has sparked a rapid development of experimental reactors in the U.S. The Department of Ene...

item.title: Ukraine targets Russian online retailer Wildberries in aerial strikes to disrupt wartime economy
item.description: Ukrainian drones have attacked multiple warehouses of Wildberries, Russia's largest online retail platform, as part of Kyiv's strategy to weaken Moscow's wartime economy. The strikes, which killed nin...

item.title: PDMA Issues Alert for Rain and Thunderstorms in Sindh
item.description: The Provincial Disaster Management Authority (PDMA) Sindh has issued a province-wide alert ahead of expected rain and thunderstorms across various parts of Sindh from July 23 to 25. Director General S...

item.title: Judge's eight-year sick leave and tribunal hearing over health claims
item.description: Judge Lepona Lekale, who has been on sick leave since 2018, appeared before the Judicial Conduct Tribunal in Sandton to address allegations of judicial incapacity. The tribunal is investigating his pr...

item.title: France-South Africa business council faces deadlock over membership disputes
item.description: The France-South Africa business council, established during Macron's 2025 visit to South Africa, has stalled progress despite initial meetings. Key issues include disputes over membership lists, with...
